preview.summary: Co-hosts Canada open their home World Cup against debutants-in-spirit Bosnia and Herzegovina in a Group B clash at BMO Field. Canada, with a settled attacking core, push the tempo, while Bosnia rely on veteran know-how and a physical front line. A bright start matters for both with Switzerland looming as the group's favorite.
preview.homeForm: Ranked around 30th by FIFA and playing all three group games on home soil under Jesse Marsch.
preview.awayForm: Ranked roughly 65th, reaching only their second World Cup after a dramatic playoff win over Italy.
preview.homeKey: Jonathan David, the goalscorer leading the line amid Davies' injury
preview.awayKey: Edin Dzeko, the 40-year-old captain and record scorer up top
storylines: Canada's first home World Cup match in front of a Toronto crowd
Alphonso Davies sidelined by a hamstring strain for the opener
Dzeko's likely last World Cup as Bosnia's talisman
preview.h2h: The two nations have rarely met, making this an unfamiliar tournament debut matchup.
